---
title: "Research Projects"
---

## Ongoing Projects

### Project: A Cognitive Mechanism Exploring of Fast-Same Effect

**Status:** Ongoing (2024 - 2026)

**Description:**
This project aims to build a neural network model incorporating cognitive processes to simulate the fast-same effect in matching tasks and explore its underlying cognitive mechanisms. The fast-same effect refers to the phenomenon where participants respond faster to "same" trials compared to "different" trials in matching tasks. By developing an artificial neural network model based on decision-making processes, we seek to better understand the cognitive mechanisms driving this effect.

**My Role:**
- Designing and implementing neural network architectures for simulating the fast-same effect
- Training and validating models against human behavioral data from matching tasks
- Analyzing model performance and interpreting results to uncover cognitive mechanisms

**Methods & Tools Used:**
- Python (PyTorch)
- Artificial Neural Networks
- Evidence Accumulation Models
- Computational Modeling
- Behavioral Data Analysis

**Outcomes:**
- Resulting paper currently in preparation

**Collaborators:**
- PI: [Hu Chuan-Peng](https://huchuanpeng.com/), School of Psychology, Nanjing Normal University

---

*Last updated: March 2026*
